title: Pre-mortem — engineer
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/engineer.md. Write risks/engineer.md in the required format. Surface 3–5 failure modes from your angle, with specific mechanism and early signal for each. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: Pre-mortem — ops
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/ops.md. Write risks/ops.md in the required format. Surface 3–5 failure modes from your angle, with specific mechanism and early signal for each. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: Pre-mortem — support
description: Read workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md, then read your brief at briefs/support.md. Write risks/support.md in the required format. Surface 3–5 failure modes from your angle, with specific mechanism and early signal for each. Follow your system prompt exactly.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Compliance / audit log integrity plan
description:
The project context requires "clean audit log for all transactions Monday onward." Your pre-mortem surfaced idempotency-key enforcement gaps and duplicate-transaction risks, but nobody explicitly owns the compliance plan.

MUST DEFINE by Friday EOD:
1. What does "clean audit log" mean for this launch? (no dups, consistent timestamps, all v1 and v2 transactions reconcilable?)
2. Post-ramp audit procedure: Monday night, how do we verify the audit log meets compliance? Who runs it? What's the pass/fail criteria?
3. Rollback data repair steps: if we roll back partway or detect dups, what's the remediation? Who owns the data repair?
4. Flag incompletely — what happens to audit log entries if rollback is incomplete? Do they get corrected?

Write audit_log_compliance_plan.md with these 4 sections and concrete steps. This is a compliance blocker for Friday ship.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: PM availability / decision authority (Thu–Mon)
description:
PM is out Tue–Wed, back Thursday. Your pre-mortem did not surface the risk: "If a critical go/no-go decision (e.g., rollback?) is needed Thursday night through Monday while PM has limited availability, who has authority to decide? How do we escalate to exec/legal if needed?"

No role can own this except as a lead escalation. You must:
1. Define decision authority for Thursday (PM back), Friday ship, Fri night–Sun ramp, Monday ramp conclusion
2. Create an escalation contact list: who in exec/legal/compliance should be notified if rollback is considered?
3. Draft a 1-paragraph "if things go wrong" decision script for ops/eng so they know when to escalate vs. when to roll back autonomously

Write decision_authority.md before Thursday. This unblocks the on-call team.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Consolidate coupon endpoint risks (testing + monitoring + briefing)
description:
Coupon endpoint /v2/coupons/apply was flagged as under-tested, unmonitored, and unbriefed across all three roles.

Engineer already proposed: production data shape audit + smoke tests.
Ops proposed: v2 dashboard panels + runbook updates.
Support proposed: 30-min briefing + error code documentation.

Your job: 
1. Absorb and consolidate these three angles into a single master action plan
2. Make sure all three are synchronized: testing schedule (engineer), monitoring readiness (ops), briefing timing (support)
3. Create a delivery checklist: "coupon endpoint ready for Friday launch" with pass/fail criteria for each workstream
4. Identify any gaps (e.g., load test sequencing, dashboard + runbook link, briefing + doc timing) and resolve them

Write consolidated_coupon_plan.md. Timeline: all deliverables done by Thursday 5 PM so support can study Friday morning.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Ramp metrics / go-no-go criteria
description:
Your pre-mortem flagged that ramp metrics are aggregated and blinded to v2 degradation (engineer + ops). This is a critical decision-gating risk: without clear stratified metrics, ops cannot make a go/no-go decision for each ramp stage (0%→25%→50%→100%).

REBALANCING NOTE: This was stated as med impact, but it gates ROLLBACK DECISION. Lack of clarity extends user pain. Bumping to HIGH priority.

YOU MUST:
1. Define the stratified metrics dashboard: v1 vs v2 side-by-side for conversion rate, latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, coupon success rate
2. Set automated alerts: if v2 error rate or latency degrades >1% vs v1 baseline, page ops-checkout
3. Define go/no-go criteria for each ramp stage: what values allow ramp to proceed? what values trigger hold/rollback?
4. Document the runbook: if alert fires during ramp, what does ops do? (escalate to eng, pause ramp, rollback?)

Write ramp_metrics_plan.md. Dashboard must be live and tested before Monday ramp.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Feedback loop / support escalation (re-rank to HIGH)
description:
Support + ops both surfaced the "silent degradation" pattern: tickets are the first signal of bugs, arriving 20–60 minutes before monitoring alerts. Your pre-mortem flagged a "feedback loop lag" risk (medium impact stated), but rebalancing shows this is actually HIGH priority — it's the CANARY signal that ops relies on if monitoring is blind.

REBALANCING: Support-to-ops escalation speed gates incident response time. Lack of a tight escalation channel means ops stays silent while customers suffer.

YOU MUST:
1. Finalize the support→ops escalation protocol: threshold (5+ tickets with same keyword in 1 hour), channel (#ops-escalation), format ("PATTERN: keyword, N tickets, first 3 attached"), response SLA (ops triages within 15 min)
2. Brief the support team on this protocol by Friday morning so they use it Sunday/Monday
3. Brief ops secondary (backend engineer) on what to do when support escalates (don't wait for primary, page ops-primary OR escalate to #ops-escalation if waiting)
4. Set up the bridge channel (#checkout-launch-ops) for the weekend where support can directly escalate without waiting for formal paging

Write support_ops_escalation_protocol.md. Protocol must be live by Friday launch.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Data reconciliation / rollback integrity
description:
Engineer flagged: flag-off rollback could fail if v1 code runs against v2-migrated schema. Also flagged: idempotency enforcement creates duplicate-transaction records during flag transitions.

But nobody surfaced the standalone risk: "If rollback is partial (some users still on v2, some on v1), how do we reconcile transaction ledgers? Do we have a post-rollback audit? What's data repair look like?"

MUST DEFINE by Friday:
1. Rollback test plan in staging: apply v2 migration, ramp v2 traffic, flip flag OFF, verify v1 transactions succeed against v2 schema, document any failures
2. Data reconciliation procedure: if rollback is partial or incomplete, how do we audit v1 vs v2 ledgers? Query templates, reconciliation tool, owner
3. Audit log correction: if we find duplicate transactions post-rollback, who owns the cleanup? (engineer + ops + compliance)
4. Rollback success criteria: after flag OFF, what queries/tests do we run to confirm ledger integrity?

Write rollback_integrity_plan.md. Rollback test must complete by Thursday EOD.

title: Post-mortem follow-up: Customer comms / rollback messaging
description:
No dedicated marketing/comms reviewer on the pre-mortem. If rollback happens Monday, what's the customer-facing message? How do we notify affected users? What if some users completed checkout before rollback?

SUPPORT owns user comms; you must:
1. Draft a rollback notification message: "We temporarily paused our new checkout experience. If you saw any issues, here's what happened and what we did..."
2. Identify who gets notified: users affected by downtime? all users? segment by region/cohort?
3. FAQ: if a user paid during the outage and got an error, what's the support response? Do we auto-refund? How do they verify?
4. Escalation path: if rollback happens, who coordinates the message? (support lead + ops + eng) and who sends it? (support comms team?)

Write rollback_comms_plan.md. Plan must be ready before Friday so support can activate quickly if needed.
